meta.title: FARMER DROP — Payment Rails: Stripe vs a Chain Transfer Coin
meta.purpose: Make the cost gap between Stripe's card rails and an L2 chain transfer coin legible, to ground Farmer Drop's phased rail strategy.
user_confirmed_facts_2026_07_04: Honey Coin is NOT ready to launch and will be ASSET-BACKED in a later phase, via the compadres asset-backed template / IReserveAdapter.
The FIRST deploy coin is a transfer-efficiency coin (CPDR-style) for cheap peer transfers — NOT the asset-backed Honey Coin.
Stripe is the launch rail because people know and trust it; it is expensive relative to a chain coin. This page makes that cost gap explicit.
These override older map language that treated Honey Coin as the near-term token.
caveats: Stripe percentage + USD-flat fees are identical US/CA; region-varying lines (international card, FX, Instant Payout minimum) use US values — the pricing session geolocated to CA.
Radar US per-screen figures ($0.05 / $0.07) come from a secondary source; verify against stripe.com/radar/pricing from a US IP before publishing.
Base ERC-20 transfer cost rests mainly on BaseScan's gas tracker; l2fees.info did not list Base at fetch time. Cross-check l2beat.com/scaling/costs.

stripe.note: US-centric. The Farmer Drop fee model already absorbs the highlighted lines: $0.55 per-txn surcharge covers Stripe's $0.30 processing + $0.25 transfer fixed legs; the 3.15% cart surcharge = 2.9% Stripe passthrough + 0.25% strategic wedge.

lines.note: Per successful charge. The core rail cost.
Add-on when the card number is typed, not presented.
US accounts. Added on non-domestic cards.
US accounts, when Stripe converts currency.
Cheaper rail, but slow settlement and reversible for ~60 days.
Pay to get funds in minutes instead of days.
Express / Custom accounts — per account that transacts that month.
The transfer leg. Farmer Drop's $0.55 surcharge covers this $0.25.
Refunded to you only if you win the dispute.
Included free on standard pricing; per-screen on interchange-plus.
Adds rules, lists, and review tooling.
0.67% for added volume on paid monthly plans.
Where you're registered to collect; $0.50/txn on the API tier.

lines.rate: No extra fee — but original fee kept
lines.note: The original 2.9% + $0.30 is NOT returned. A refunded $100 sale still cost you $3.20.

base_rail.note: Post-EIP-4844 (blobs) collapsed L2 data-availability cost. A token transfer is now sub-cent to low-cents.

honest_additional_costs.detail: USDC mint/redeem (Circle 1:1, larger sizes) or exchange spread ~0.1–0.5%. Modeled here at 0.1% per crossing. This is where 'chain is free' quietly stops being true.
Seed-phrase safety, wallet UX, recovery, and (for the platform) a Safe multisig admin key — a real operating cost, not a per-transfer fee.
KYC/AML posture, whitelist-only pilot (compadres ENABLE_CPDR_WHITELIST), reconciliation, and legal classification — fixed monthly cost amortized across volume.

model.cpdr_marginal_usd: 0.006  (gas only, once funds are on-chain, via TransferRouter)

scenarios.label: $100 marketplace cart
scenarios.member_charged_note: 3.15% cart surcharge + $0.55; Stripe takes $3.45, platform keeps $0.25 wedge

crossover.note: On marginal (per-transfer) cost the chain undercuts Stripe from the first dollar — Stripe's $0.55 fixed leg alone is ~90× a Base transfer. What Stripe buys you is trust and zero setup; the chain front-loads custody + compliance + ramp liquidity. The crossover is a monthly-overhead break-even, not a per-transfer one.

phases.name: Stripe rails
phases.detail: Fiat + credits ledger on Stripe. People know and trust it; onboarding is instant and no custody burden. Cost is high (2.9% + $0.55) but bounded, and the fee model absorbs it via $0.55 + 3.15%.

phases.name: CPDR-style transfer coin
phases.detail: The compadres harness (CPDR): TransferRouter for routed peer transfers with clientOrderId, mock USDC funding, primary issuance, toy AMM (5 bps), burn/redeem, fee measurement and chain==event==DB==UI reconciliation. This is the FIRST real card-rail cost removal — the only mechanism that genuinely drops Stripe's fee, not the PMA 'tax' framing.
phases.warning: Whitelist-only mainnet pilot; admin key must be a Safe multisig; exact approvals; no honey/asset-backing yet.

phases.name: Asset-backed Honey Coin
phases.detail: Enters through compadres docs/06_asset_backed_template.md and IReserveAdapter (reserveUnitsAvailable / reserveUnitsCommitted / canMint). Redeemable digital honey CREDIT — claim against specified inventory, burn-to-redeem, published inventory snapshots. NOT an investment/profit-share token.
phases.warning: REGULATORY CLASSIFICATION REQUIRED FIRST: a USD-redeemable, food-spendable, USD-boosted token sits in stored-value / money-transmission / possibly-securities territory. Counsel before launch, not after (map §4 ⚠).
